title: News Releases | National Institutes of Health (NIH)
content: News Releases Media Inquiries NIH on Social Media Images and B-roll Calendar of Events NIH News Publications News Releases Search NIH news releases Search Filter by Year (optional) - Any - 2025 2024 2023 2022 2021 2020 2019 2018 NIH-funded AI model predicts cancer survival from single-cell tumor data April 21, 2026 — Study links specific cell populations to patient risk in melanoma and liver cancer. Dr. Jonathan M. Green selected as CEO of the NIH Clinical Center April 21, 2026 — The NIH Clinical Center is the largest research hospital in the world. NIH-funded breakthrough shrinks CRISPR for precision delivery in the body April 13, 2026 — Smaller gene-editing system could expand treatment options for cancer, ALS and other diseases. Low-cost care model reduces blood pressure in high-risk populations April 8, 2026 — NIH-supported clinical trial shows coordinated care strategy more effective than standard care NIH researchers discover pain-relieving drug with minimal addictive properties April 1, 2026 — Positive safety profile of novel drug compound is surprise for class of synthetic opioids shelved years ago. NIH awards top scientific teams for innovations linking nutrition and autoimmune disease March 30, 2026 — Competition sought bold ideas to better understand how dietary interventions could influence disease onset and symptom management. Dr. Elisabeth Armstrong named NIH Chief of Staff March 30, 2026 — She will provide strategic counsel to the NIH Director and other key leaders within NIH, in addition to managing process, operations, and information flows. Chronic inflammation leaves long-lasting impression on gut stem cells, increasing colorectal cancer risk March 25, 2026 — NIH-funded animal study finds heritable memories of damage persisted in cells months after inflammation ceased. NIH invests $150 million in human-based research to reduce use of animal models March 18, 2026 — New program will develop, validate and standardize research tools to develop more sophisticated and relevant models of disease. Clinical trial results support use of weekly extended-release buprenorphine for treatment of opioid use disorder during pregnancy March 16, 2026 — NIH-supported study shows this treatment resulted in higher rates of illicit opioid abstinence than current standard of care. For the Press Media Contacts NIH-Funded Research (EurekAlert.org) Pick-up Research Articles ( NIH Research Matters ) Pick-up Health Articles ( NIH News in Health ) More sources for NIH news and events Connect with Us Subscribe to news releases RSS Feed This page last reviewed on March 10, 2025
